title: [CI:DOCS] Fix handling of shadow-utils
body:
There seems to be a bug in rpm, where it fails silently if you specify
rpm --restore --quiet shadow-utils.

rpm --restore shadow-utils 2> /dev/null

Does the right thing.

[NO TESTS NEEDED] Might add tests from buildah, once we have them
working correctly.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

This should work, but `--setcaps` doesn't produce error messages that need to be silenced, so it may be a better choice.
I was going with --restore, since it seemed closer to the reinstall.  IE it would fix other potential issues in the permissions of the package.

LGTM
and happy green test buttons
